Metadata.title: VisTwin — A digital twin platform pioneering the built environment
Metadata.description: A digital twin platform for the built environment. We pull what BIM, simulation, sensors, and operational systems each say separately into one shared spatial substrate — for building management, safety, smart factories, and spatial AI.

Hero.tagline: A digital twin platform for the built environment. We pull what BIM, simulation, sensors, and operations are each saying separately into one shared spatial substrate — so every decision carries a coordinate, evidence, and a traceable record.

OneLiner.text: An AI-driven digital twin platform for the built environment. Simulation and operations woven into one shared working system that spans a building's full lifecycle.

Capabilities.title: Three layers, one spatial substrate.
Capabilities.intro: We're not a rendering studio. We're not a BIM consultancy. We build the operating layer for the built environment's digital twin — simulation outputs, sensor streams, and operational commands, all on one spatial coordinate system. The three layers are mutually visible, mutually writable, and mutually queryable.

items.name: Simulation
items.description: Concept-stage physics that used to take days now resolves in seconds — wind, sunlight, circulation, energy, pedestrian comfort — usable while the architect is still in the room. Every computed value is written back to the spatial substrate, queryable later just like a live sensor reading.

items.name: Sensor twin
items.description: Sensors, BMS, and ESG metrics are not aggregated into a chart somewhere — they're written directly onto spatial nodes. Temperature, occupancy, CO₂, energy, structural state, indoor air — readable at the exact coordinate where each thing happens.

items.name: System bridge
items.description: A read isn't the end — decisions propagate to FM work orders, CMMS scheduling, BMS control, PLC, edge actuators. The spatial substrate isn't read-only; it's the trigger surface for the entire operations stack. The read becomes a write back into the world.

Position.title: Honest about the gap we fill
Position.intro: Every actor in the built environment sells their own slice — the architect sells visuals, the BIM consultancy sells reports, the IoT platform sells dashboards, the FM platform sells tickets. The same building ends up sliced four ways and stored on four different consultants' drives. We sit in the gap none of them fill — a shared substrate living in space, where those fragments finally sit on the same coordinate system, visible, queryable, and capable of triggering the next action.

rows.ours: A shared substrate living in space

rows.theirs: BIM put on a hard drive after handover

rows.ours: ESG sourced from live IoT sensors, streamed in
rows.theirs: ESG sourced from quarterly Excel surveys

rows.ours: Sensor data lives at the coordinate it happens
rows.theirs: Sensor data lives in dashboard charts

rows.ours: One coordinate system across the whole lifecycle
rows.theirs: A new model rebuilt for every phase

Architecture.title: Data · Logic · System — deciding, in space.
Architecture.intro: Up to this point we've talked about real problems in the built environment. But look one layer deeper — this logic isn't unique to buildings. Any field sliced across multiple systems needs a layer where data, logic, and operations speak on the same coordinate. This is what ontology means in space: DATA enters, LOGIC computes, SYSTEM acts — the syllogism closes inside one spatial frame. Decisions acquire location. Responsibility acquires a trace. The entire field becomes thinkable as one thing. From building management to safety, smart factory to AI-driven spatial decisioning — the same ontology underwrites every domain that needs system-level judgment.

axes.label: DATA
axes.name: Facts in
axes.examples: MQTT · OPC UA · BIM · BMS · ESG metrics · sensors
axes.description: Reality enters — sensor readings, BMS values, ESG metrics, BIM geometry, energy, structural state. Every fact carries a timestamp and a spatial address, living at the place it actually occurred.

axes.label: LOGIC
axes.name: Computation
axes.examples: CFD · Solar · WELL · LEED · EEWH · ML
axes.description: Reasoning over facts — CFD, ML models, certification scoring, rule engines — written back as first-class derived properties. The computed becomes as queryable as the measured.

axes.label: SYSTEM
axes.name: Action
axes.description: Decisions reached in the ontology propagate back out to the systems that actually move things — FM tickets, CMMS scheduling, BMS control, PLC, edge actuators. Judgment becomes action on the ground.

spatial.label: SPATIAL · USD Prim binding
spatial.footnote: The syllogism closes in space: DATA → LOGIC → SYSTEM, all standing on coordinates defined by USD Prims. This is the layer other ontology platforms structurally lack — without location, decisions have no traceable record.

Architecture.centerSubtitle: A decision substrate shared across buildings, factories, and sites · maps to BIM · ESG · WELL · LEED · EEWH · IFC · ISO 19650
Architecture.footnote: Designed in v0.1 · first commercial pilot Q4 2026.

Standards.tagline: We don't reinvent the standards. We align to international practice — and speak the same language as your review process. EEWH, LEED, IFC, ISO, ASHRAE: the reports line up.

Surface.title: Decisions, forming
Surface.intro: Not mockups. These are windows live inside Omniverse Kit — the operating layer is built and running. Massing studies, glass ratios, pedestrian wind, per-unit daylight, factory energy, site personnel, FM tickets — behind every frame, an architect, a developer, a plant manager, or a facilities engineer is making a real call right now.

anchor.alt: VisTwin suite running inside Omniverse Kit
anchor.name: Live suite
anchor.specs: Omniverse Kit · nine families online: air / sun / water / carbon / safety / data / intelligence / stage / core

tiles.name: Wind Tunnel
tiles.specs: Potential flow · 1200 particles · NVIDIA Warp · flow around three masses

tiles.name: Solar Heatmap
tiles.specs: Per-unit integration · 180 units · NOAA SPA ±0.0003° · daily 24h sweep

tiles.name: Wind Environment Report
tiles.specs: Lawson pedestrian classes · z=150cm · 100×100 grid · export-ready

Surface.footnote: These are surface. What runs underneath is one schema, shared across industries.

items.title: Building object schema v0.1
items.description: TBox for the built environment — spaces, systems, equipment, sensors, work orders. Pydantic-first, USD-aware. The single shared definition the rest of the platform builds on.

items.title: Live property pipeline
items.description: MQTT and OPC UA into Postgres + property graph. Sub-second updates from sensors and BMS land directly on the building object — no dashboard middleman.

items.title: Derived property pipeline
items.description: GPU-accelerated CFD, solar, and occupancy ML. Outputs written back as Derived Properties on the same object. The consumer cannot tell which is measured and which is computed.

items.title: Spatial binding layer
items.description: Bidirectional binding between Ontology Object and USD Prim on an Omniverse stage. Click the Prim, get the Object. Update the Object, the Prim updates.

items.title: First commercial deployment
items.description: End-to-end pilot with a Taipei development partner — full stack from sensor ingest, through derived properties, to spatial readout in a live building.

items.name: Operations & maintenance
items.description: After construction ends, BIM stops being a static file — it becomes a living model, queried daily. Sensor readings, structural state, certification scores, and work-order events all bound to spatial nodes. Design-stage decisions stay traceable two decades into operation and decommission.

items.name: Property management
items.description: Resident tickets, common-area status, energy allocation, visitor flow — all bound back to the actual floor plan. The property team stops thumbing through spreadsheets; they point at the model to see which unit reported a fault, which fixture needs maintenance, which corridor has the heaviest traffic.

items.name: Safety monitoring
items.description: On-site and on-floor personnel, equipment, and environment — all aligned to the model's geometry. Visual detection, fall and pose recognition, zone breaches, PPE compliance — risk is no longer understood after an incident, but pointed at in space, in real time.

items.name: Smart factory
items.description: Lines, equipment, energy, logistics flow, worker safety — all bound back to the floor. One coordinate system; the line manager, the energy engineer, and the safety lead share the same field. Bottlenecks, anomalies, and savings get pointed at in space, not buried at the intersection of ten reports.

CTA.headline: Give decisions a coordinate.
CTA.description: What we deliver isn't a render — it's a decision substrate that lives in space. The whole stack and our team, deployed into your building, plant, site, or any field that needs system-level judgment.

Contact.title: Tell us what you are building
Contact.subtitle: We reply to every message — usually within a working day.
Contact.name: Name
Contact.success: Message received. We will be in touch shortly.

Cases.title: The system, coming into focus
Cases.subtitle: An ontology forms before it ships. These are the components currently on the active surface — positioned by where each sits on the trajectory from design intent to first deployment.
